missing_or_unclear_items: hardware set referenced by doors but not found in spec

general_notes: 1. PROVIDE CAULK / SEALANT ON BOTH SIDES OF FRAMES, TYPICAL
2. PROVIDE TEMPERED GLAZING AT ALL STOREFRONT DOORS, AT NEW GLAZING WITHIN 24" HORIZONTALLY FROM A DOOR, WHERE NEW GLAZING LITE IS WITHIN 18" A.F.F.
3. FIELD VERIFY ALL CONDITIONS PRIOR TO ORDERING DOORS, HARDWARE, OR ACCESS CONTROLS, TYPICAL.
4. COORDINATE THRESHOLDS WITH NEW / EXISTING FLOOR FINISHES
5. NOTIFY ARCHITECT OF ANY DISCREPANCIES BETWEEN DOOR SCHEDULE AND PLAN AND/OR EXISTING CONDITIONS.

6. ENTIRE DOOR ASSEMBLIES TO MAINTAIN RATINGS AS SCHEDULED AND SHOWN ON THE LIFE SAFETY SHEETS. PROVIDE HARDWARE FOR RATED ASSEMBLIES TO MEET ALL APPLICABLE CODES TO INCLUDE BUT NOT LIMITED TO CLOSERS, PANIC DEVICES, CAULK / SEALANT, SWEEPS, SMOKE SEALS, ETC.
7. SET ALL THRESHOLDS IN EXTERIOR DOORS IN FULL BED OF SEALANT.
8. ALL EXTERIOR DOORS TO MEET WATER TIGHT CONDITION INCLUDING ALL WATERPROOF THRESHOLDS. BUMPER SEALS REQUIRED AT ALL EXTERIOR DOORS U.N.O.
9. SEE DOOR HARDWARE SPECIFICATION FOR DOOR HARDWARE.
10. COORDINATE ALL DOOR HARDWARE WITH ACCESS CONTROL AND FIRE ALARM SYSTEM AS REQUIRED.
11. SCOPE OF WORK: ALL NEW FINISHED DOOR FRAMES SHALL BE FINISHED AS SPECIFIED ON PLANS AND PROJECT SPECIFICATIONS.
12. ALL HARDWARE SHALL MATCH PNC STANDARD.
13. ALL DOORS, FRAMES, AND HARDWARE, INCLUDING THOSE OF DIFFERING SIZE, MATERIAL, AND OPERATION SHALL BE SUPPLIED BY THE CONTRACTOR.
14. ALL KEYING SHALL BE BY GENERAL CONTRACTOR. VERIFY THE NUMBER OF KEYS REQUIRED FOR THIS PROJECT WITH THE OWNER.
15. DOOR ASSEMBLIES SHALL BE FIRE RATED ("LABELED") WHEN REQUIRED BY THE LOCAL BUILDING CODE.

16. G.C. TO PROTECT PREFINISHED DOORS AND FRAMES THROUGH THE CONSTRUCTION PERIOD. DOORS THAT ARE DAMAGED AS A RESULT OF THEM OR THEIR SUBCONTRACTOR'S WORK DURING THIS PERIOD SHALL BE REPAIRED OR REPLACED BY THE G.C.
17. BIDDING CONTRACTORS SHALL VERIFY ALL INFORMATION IN REGARDS TO DOOR ASSEMBLIES, BUILDING STANDARDS (SIZES, VENEERS, FRAME TYPES, ETC.) AND RESPONSIBILITIES PRIOR TO STARTING BIDS.

18. ALL DOOR HARDWARE SHALL BE A.D.A ACCESSIBLE. ALL DOORS SHALL COMPLY WITH ICC/ANSI 404 AND SHALL HAVE LEVER OR EQUAL HARDWARE. ALL CLOSERS MUST BE ADJUSTED TO REQUIRE NO MORE THAN 8.5 LBF FOR INTERIOR DOORS AND 5.0 FOR EXTERIOR DOORS.
19. COORDINATE ALL DOORS WITH SECURITY VENDOR.
A. DOOR INSTALLED BY VENDOR. REFER TO MATRIX FOR VENDOR CONTACT INFORMATION.
B. PROVIDE (2) 180° PEEP SIGHTS. ONE TO BE INSTALLED IN CENTER OF DOOR AT 5' - 0" A.F.F. ONE TO BE INSTALLED IN CENTER OF DOOR AT 3' - 7" A.F.F.
C. DOOR TO HAVE 1" UNDERCUT.
D. ACCESS CONTROL PUSH BUTTONS SHALL BE ON CORRIDOR SIDE OF DOOR.
E. NO THRESHOLD AT INTERIOR AUTOMATIC SLIDING DOORS.
F. DOOR FRAME TO MATCH HAWORTH DEMOUNTABLE FRAME FINISH.
G. APPLY LLUMAR SAFETY-AND-SECURITY FILM ON DOOR GLAZING. INSTALL PER MANUFACTURER'S INSTRUCTIONS.
H. PAINT DOOR TO MATCH ADJACENT WALL FINISH.
I. PROVIDE 3-WAY SWITCH AND LOW VOLTAGE WIRE TO BE ROUTED FROM CONCIERGE STATIONS TO ABOVE THE STANLEY DOORS (ONE PER STANLEY DOOR).
J. BULLET RESISTANT DOOR PROVIDED BY OTHERS.
hardware_preamble: 1. PROVIDE CAULK / SEALANT ON BOTH SIDES OF FRAMES, TYPICAL
2. PROVIDE TEMPERED GLAZING AT ALL STOREFRONT DOORS, AT NEW GLAZING WITHIN 24" HORIZONTALLY FROM A DOOR, WHERE NEW GLAZING LITE IS WITHIN 18" A.F.F.
3. FIELD VERIFY ALL CONDITIONS PRIOR TO ORDERING DOORS, HARDWARE, OR ACCESS CONTROLS, TYPICAL.
4. COORDINATE THRESHOLDS WITH NEW / EXISTING FLOOR FINISHES
5. NOTIFY ARCHITECT OF ANY DISCREPANCIES BETWEEN DOOR SCHEDULE AND PLAN AND/OR EXISTING CONDITIONS.

6. ENTIRE DOOR ASSEMBLIES TO MAINTAIN RATINGS AS SCHEDULED AND SHOWN ON THE LIFE SAFETY SHEETS. PROVIDE HARDWARE FOR RATED ASSEMBLIES TO MEET ALL APPLICABLE CODES TO INCLUDE BUT NOT LIMITED TO CLOSERS, PANIC DEVICES, CAULK / SEALANT, SWEEPS, SMOKE SEALS, ETC.
7. SET ALL THRESHOLDS IN EXTERIOR DOORS IN FULL BED OF SEALANT.
8. ALL EXTERIOR DOORS TO MEET WATER TIGHT CONDITION INCLUDING ALL WATERPROOF THRESHOLDS. BUMPER SEALS REQUIRED AT ALL EXTERIOR DOORS U.N.O.
9. SEE DOOR HARDWARE SPECIFICATION FOR DOOR HARDWARE.
10. COORDINATE ALL DOOR HARDWARE WITH ACCESS CONTROL AND FIRE ALARM SYSTEM AS REQUIRED.
11. SCOPE OF WORK: ALL NEW FINISHED DOOR FRAMES SHALL BE FINISHED AS SPECIFIED ON PLANS AND PROJECT SPECIFICATIONS.
12. ALL HARDWARE SHALL MATCH PNC STANDARD.
13. ALL DOORS, FRAMES, AND HARDWARE, INCLUDING THOSE OF DIFFERING SIZE, MATERIAL, AND OPERATION SHALL BE SUPPLIED BY THE CONTRACTOR.
15. DOOR ASSEMBLIES SHALL BE FIRE RATED ("LABELED") WHEN REQUIRED BY THE LOCAL BUILDING CODE.

16. G.C. TO PROTECT PREFINISHED DOORS AND FRAMES THROUGH THE CONSTRUCTION PERIOD. DOORS THAT ARE DAMAGED AS A RESULT OF THEM OR THEIR SUBCONTRACTOR'S WORK DURING THIS PERIOD SHALL BE REPAIRED OR REPLACED BY THE G.C.
17. BIDDING CONTRACTORS SHALL VERIFY ALL INFORMATION IN REGARDS TO DOOR ASSEMBLIES, BUILDING STANDARDS (SIZES, VENEERS, FRAME TYPES, ETC.) AND RESPONSIBILITIES PRIOR TO STARTING BIDS.

18. ALL DOOR HARDWARE SHALL BE A.D.A ACCESSIBLE. ALL DOORS SHALL COMPLY WITH ICC/ANSI 404 AND SHALL HAVE LEVER OR EQUAL HARDWARE. ALL CLOSERS MUST BE ADJUSTED TO REQUIRE NO MORE THAN 8.5 LBF FOR INTERIOR DOORS AND 5.0 FOR EXTERIOR DOORS.
19. COORDINATE ALL DOORS WITH SECURITY VENDOR.
A. DOOR INSTALLED BY VENDOR. REFER TO MATRIX FOR VENDOR CONTACT INFORMATION.
B. PROVIDE (2) 180° PEEP SIGHTS. ONE TO BE INSTALLED IN CENTER OF DOOR AT 5' - 0" A.F.F. ONE TO BE INSTALLED IN CENTER OF DOOR AT 3' - 7" A.F.F.
C. DOOR TO HAVE 1" UNDERCUT.
D. ACCESS CONTROL PUSH BUTTONS SHALL BE ON CORRIDOR SIDE OF DOOR.
E. NO THRESHOLD AT INTERIOR AUTOMATIC SLIDING DOORS.
F. DOOR FRAME TO MATCH HAWORTH DEMOUNTABLE FRAME FINISH.
G. APPLY LLUMAR SAFETY-AND-SECURITY FILM ON DOOR GLAZING. INSTALL PER MANUFACTURER'S INSTRUCTIONS.
H. PAINT DOOR TO MATCH ADJACENT WALL FINISH.
I. PROVIDE 3-WAY SWITCH AND LOW VOLTAGE WIRE TO BE ROUTED FROM CONCIERGE STATIONS TO ABOVE THE STANLEY DOORS (ONE PER STANLEY DOOR).
J. BULLET RESISTANT DOOR PROVIDED BY OTHERS.
** COORDINATE ALL SIGNAGE DESIGN AND FINAL PLACEMENT WITH SIGNAGE VENDOR
keying_notes: 14. ALL KEYING SHALL BE BY GENERAL CONTRACTOR. VERIFY THE NUMBER OF KEYS REQUIRED FOR THIS PROJECT WITH THE OWNER.
** NOTE: ALL LOCKING HARDWARE MUST BE SUPPLIED AND PREPARED TO ACCEPT 7-PIN SMALL FORMAT INTERCHANGEBLE CORES (SFIC) UNLESS NOTED OTHERWISE.**

supply_only_notes: Submittal package: door, frame and hardware schedules with manufacturer cut sheets, keying schedule, electrified hardware riser, anchor templates.
installation_only_notes: Field-measure all openings before fabrication release. Verify rough-opening size, frame anchor type, and floor condition.
Storage and protection of doors / frames / hardware on site is by GC unless otherwise noted.
exclusions_to_consider: Exclude 120 V power, low-voltage cabling, access-control head-end, intrusion detection programming, fire-alarm tie-in (NIC).
Exclude permits, demolition, painting, drywall patching at frame anchors, floor preparation at thresholds.
coordination_items: Attend pre-installation meeting with GC, EC, low-voltage, fire alarm and security trades.
Hold formal keying meeting with owner before cylinder fabrication.
